title: Add admin view for reviewing submitted payments

requirements.summary: Create AdminPage component that fetches and displays all submitted payment records in a styled table with masked card numbers
acceptanceCriteria: An AdminPage component exists and renders a table of all submitted payment records.
Each row displays: Full Name, Email, Address, masked Card Number (last 4 digits only), Expiry Date, and Amount.
The page fetches data from the backend `getAllPayments` query method via a React Query hook.
The table is styled consistently with the existing light theme (card surfaces, amber accents, neutral text).
A loading state is shown while data is being fetched.
An empty state message is shown when no submissions exist.
The admin view is accessible via a dedicated route or navigation link in the app header.

file_operations.description: Create AdminPage component that uses the useGetAllPayments hook to fetch payment records and displays them in a table. Include a sticky header with app branding and a 'Back to Payment' navigation link. Display Full Name, Email, Address, masked Card Number (last 4 digits only), Expiry Date, and Amount in table columns. Apply the existing light theme styling with card surfaces (white/off-white), amber/gold accents, and neutral typography. Show a loading spinner while fetching data. Show an empty state message when no payments exist. Use the same layout structure and styling patterns as PaymentPage for consistency.

requirements.summary: Add React Query hook for fetching all payment records from the backend
acceptanceCriteria: A `useGetAllPayments` (or equivalent) React Query hook exists in `useQueries.ts`.
The hook calls the backend `getAllPayments` canister query method.
The hook returns loading, error, and data states consumable by AdminPage.

file_operations.description: Add a useGetAllPayments React Query hook that calls the backend getAllPayments actor method. The hook should return an array of Payment objects with loading, error, and data states. Use the useActor hook to get the backend actor instance. Configure the query with appropriate stale time and refetch settings for admin data review.

requirements.summary: Add routing support and Admin navigation link to enable navigation between PaymentPage and AdminPage
acceptanceCriteria: The app has at least two navigable views: the payment form (default) and the admin view.
A clearly labeled 'Admin' link appears in the sticky header.
Clicking the Admin link renders the AdminPage.
The PaymentPage remains the default landing view.

file_operations.description: Replace the single PaymentPage render with a simple client-side routing mechanism (using React state or hash-based routing) that renders PaymentPage by default and AdminPage when the admin route is active. Ensure PaymentPage is the default landing view.

Add an 'Admin' navigation link in the sticky header that triggers navigation to the admin view. Style the link consistently with the existing light theme (amber/gold accent on hover). Position the link prominently in the header alongside the existing branding.
